title: Tomato bacterial wilt + virus complex — no chemical control
body: For tomato bacterial wilt (Ralstonia solanacearum), bacterial canker (Clavibacter michiganensis subsp. michiganensis), and the four important virus complexes — TYLCV (whitefly-transmitted), Curly Top Virus (leafhopper), Tomato Spotted Wilt Virus (thrips), and Cucumber Mosaic Virus (aphid) — HORDI confirms there is no direct chemical control. Management is consolidated: use resistant / disease-free varieties, rotate with non-solanaceous crops, keep the field weed-free, practice field sanitation, and suppress insect vectors with DOA-approved insecticides.
quote: Use disease free seeds; Weed free field and field sanitation; Reduce vector population (By insecticides).
